Detects modification of the registry settings used for Internet Explorer and other Windows components that use these settings. An attacker can abuse this registry key to add a domain to the trusted sites Zone or insert JavaScript for persistence

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: This sample is a functional Remote Access Trojan (RAT) or Backdoor. It exhibits several high-confidence malicious behaviors: 1) Persistence: It modifies the 'Run' registry key and creates a masqueraded shortcut ('Internet Explorer Update.lnk') in the Windows Startup folder. 2) Credential Theft: It targets 'pstorec.dll' to call 'PStoreCreateInstance', a legacy technique for stealing credentials stored in Protected Storage. 3) C2 Communication: It beacons to 'video.csmcpr.com' using hardcoded User-Agents and processes bot commands such as 'get', 'run', 'sleep', and 'dos'. 4) Remote Shell: It implements a reverse shell by creating pipes and redirecting standard I/O for spawned processes. 5) Information Gathering: It fingerprints the system, collecting the OS version, username, and proxy settings.
